figures.caption: Figure 1: Implied volatility smile for the baseline one month experiment. The green line is the flat Black-Scholes volatility used to define the GBM world, the orange markers are GBM Monte Carlo prices, and the blue markers are RN-DDPM prices.
Table 2: Multi-strike call prices and implied volatilities for the baseline configuration. Prices are in currency units, volatilities are annualized.

Table 1: Single-strike diagnostics for a one month maturity. RN-DDPM refers to sampling with the epsilon shift under the risk neutral measure Q. GBM refers to the analytic or Monte Carlo reference in the GBM world.
Table 4: DDPM vs. GBM prices for discrete arithmetic Asian call options. Results computed using N = 20,000 Monte Carlo paths for each method. Standard errors in parentheses.

Figure 2: Martingale diagnostic. The blue curve shows Mt = e−rtE[St] for the RN-DDPM sampler. The orange curve shows the same quantity for the no-shift DDPM. The dashed line marks the initial spot S0.
